title: pyrus × floribunda
intro: Pyrus × floribunda, also known as the flowering pear, is a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Pyrus communis and Pyrus ussuriensis. This plant is valued for its ornamental features, with showy white flowers and attractive foliage. The bark and leaves of the flowering pear have been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asian cultures, for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's extracts are used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to offer health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting cardiovascular well-being.
